text:
rk of time
and space. Even a good work suffers sometimes if it be done at a
wrong time. Similar is the case of uncongenial environments. We
are reminded in this connection of Kalidusa's remark to the
effect that a submission made at an appropriate time by a servant
to the master bears fruit immediately. The importance of time is
recognised by all human beings on the basis of their experience
in life. Now who is responsible for time? We know that it is the
Sun, who, though situated thousands of miles away from our
earth, causes days and nights as well as seasons by his apparent
motions. For, the denizens of this terrestrial sphere consider the
Sun's movements, rising, moving to the zenith of the sky and
setting and disappearing and causing nights, as real, though the
Vedas declare that the Sun never rises, nor sets. He is visible
always to the inner eye of the enlightened Seers: "Sada pasyanti
surayah, diviva caksur atatam". "Seers perceive Him always.
